step.action: Scan the codebase for architecture patterns, components, layers, and module boundaries.
Produce an architecture map documenting the current system structure.
Catalog all APIs, endpoints, contracts, and integration points in the codebase.
Document request/response shapes, authentication, and versioning.
Map all dependencies: packages, versions, licenses, and transitive dependencies.
Flag outdated, vulnerable, or problematic dependencies.
Map existing UI flows, screens, navigation patterns, and user journeys.
Document component hierarchy and state management patterns.
Assess non-functional requirement baselines: performance, security, accessibility, reliability.
Use the architecture map to identify NFR-relevant components and measure current state.
Synthesize all scan results into a gap-focused PRD.
Identify gaps between current state and desired state.
step.template-output: Apply the template from template.md, filling all sections with scan findings and gap analysis.
